case_name: Riedel v. Akron Gen. Health Sys.

summary.brief: Riedel v. Akron Gen. Health Sys.. Medical malpractice case involving paediatrics.

evidence_index.text: Appellees complained that the untimely diagnosis of Riedel's spinal epidural abscess was the cause of Riedel's incomplete paraplegia.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Dr. Kalapodis testified that, in spite of the presence of an active methicillin-resistant staphylococcus aureus ('MRSA') infection that was causing Riedel severe back pain, he failed to diagnose the abscess during Riedel's May 22, 2013 emergency room visit.
evidence_index.speaker: Dr. Kalapodis

evidence_index.text: On June 24, 2016, the jury issued a verdict against appellants, jointly and severally. Riedel was awarded $5,200,000 in economic damages, but no noneconomic damages.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The trial court's order is affirmed.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

evidence_index.text: Appellees' experts Darlene Carruthers ('Carruthers'), a life care plan expert, and economist John Burke, Ph.D. ('Burke'), testified to the scope and present value of the life care plan respectively.
evidence_index.speaker: Appellees' experts

evidence_index.text: Riedel testified that Lodi was close to his daughters' home and he was seeking emergency medical care.
evidence_index.speaker: Riedel

evidence_index.text: We find that the jury's verdict is not against the manifest weight of the evidence pursuant to Civ.R. 59(A)(6) as it is supported by competent, credible evidence.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

evidence_index.text: The jury, as the trier of fact, 'was free to accept or reject any or all of appellants' evidence relating to damages.'
evidence_index.speaker: Court reasoning

evidence_index.text: Appellants' expert, John Scarbrough, Ph.D. ('Scarbrough'), provided a range of life expectancy and estimate quotes, and determined that the life care plan with a life expectancy of 78 years totaled $4,654,038.
evidence_index.speaker: Appellants' expert

evidence_index.text: The trial court denied appellants' motion to set-off economic damages under the ACA.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

simulation.testable_reason: The case contains a real patient presentation with clinical facts, a clinical decision where a healthcare provider chose a course of action, and a discussion of what the standard of care required.

initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Patient presented with severe back pain and was later found to have an active MRSA infection.

focused_exam.back: Severe pain noted, further details not specified

requestables.name: MRI of the spine
requestables.description: Imaging to evaluate for spinal epidural abscess

reveal.clinical_significance: Would have identified spinal epidural abscess
reveal.decision_impact: Critical for timely diagnosis and treatment

timeline_phases.name: Emergency Presentation
timeline_phases.description: Patient presents to ED with severe back pain

timeline_phases.name: Initial Workup
timeline_phases.description: Initial assessment and decision-making in ED

decision_points.clinical_context: Patient in ED with severe back pain and active MRSA infection
decision_points.prompt: What diagnostic test should be ordered to evaluate the cause of back pain?

options.clinical_reasoning: MRI is the standard investigation to rule out spinal epidural abscess.

options.description: Do not order MRI, manage symptomatically
options.clinical_reasoning: Failed to diagnose the underlying cause of severe back pain.

expected_action_court.description: MRI of the spine should have been ordered
expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Expert testimony indicated MRI was necessary to diagnose spinal epidural abscess.

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Failure to order MRI led to delayed diagnosis of spinal epidural abscess.
explanation.what_should_have_happened: MRI should have been performed to confirm diagnosis.
explanation.legal_standard_applied: Standard of care as established by expert testimony

patient_outcome.description: Patient developed incomplete paraplegia due to delayed diagnosis of spinal epidural abscess.

key_findings: Breach of duty in failing to diagnose spinal epidural abscess
Causation established - delayed diagnosis led to paraplegia

malpractice_determination.counterfactual: MRI would have shown spinal epidural abscess

factual_timeline.event: Patient presented to ED with severe back pain

allegations.allegation: Failure to diagnose spinal epidural abscess

court_findings.legal_significance: Failure to diagnose spinal epidural abscess
